# 2023 Hydrogen Levelized Cost Update: Green Beats Gray

![](https://assets.bbhub.io/image/v1/convert?type=auto&url=https%3A%2F%2Fassets.bbhub.io%2Fprofessional%2Fsites%2F24%2F399736907.jpg)

July 25, 2023

By Adithya Bhashyam, Associate, Hydrogen, BloombergNEF

The levelized cost of hydrogen (LCOH2) has risen slightly in 2023 due to inflation and higher financing costs. Still, green hydrogen (H2) will become competitive with existing gray H2 plants running at marginal cost in five markets by 2030.

# Levelized Cost of Energy+ (LCOE+)

![]()
![](/media/d4wcvrp5/lcoe-may2024-header-logo-only.jpg?width=768&height=307&upscale=false)
![]()
![](/media/d4wcvrp5/lcoe-may2024-header-logo-only.jpg?width=768&height=432&upscale=false)

Lazard's Levelized Cost of Energy+ (LCOE+) is a widely-cited, annual analysis that provides insights into the cost competitiveness of various energy generation technologies. Now in its 18th year, the report explores key aspects of energy generation, energy storage, and system-level considerations while reflecting on developments over the past 12 months.

## Access the 2025 LCOE+ Report

Lazard's 2025 LCOE+ report highlights that, despite headwinds and macroeconomic challenges, renewables remain the most cost-competitive form of new-build generation on an unsubsidized basis (i.e., without tax subsidies). As such, renewable energy will continue to play a key role in the buildout of new power generation in the U.S. This is particularly true in the current high power demand environment, where renewables stand out as both the lowest-cost and quickest-to-deploy generation resource. The report also emphasizes the need for diverse generation fleets to meet rising power demands, as well as the vital role system-wide planning and innovation will play in shaping a reliable and sustainable energy future.

![]()
![](/media/txep5o1j/lcoe-website-banner-gif-5.gif?width=768&height=432&upscale=false)

The results of our 2025 analyses underscore the resilience of renewables amid macroeconomic pressures, while emphasizing the critical need for diverse generation portfolios and innovation to meet surging power demands and ensure grid reliability.

![]()
![](/media/1qnb3p44/bilicic-george-1200x1200.jpg?width=768&height=768&upscale=false)

While our 2025 analyses showcase the continued cost competitiveness of renewables and the importance of diversified energy portfolios, the broader Energy Transition remains shaped by dynamic factors—technological innovation, policy evolution, and market conditions—that represent both tremendous opportunities and complex challenges for the industry.

![]()
![](/media/dfqgmpyf/scroggins-samuel-1200x1200.jpg?width=768&height=768&upscale=false)

## Download Lazard's LCOE+ 2025 Report

SUMMARY:
The Global Hydrogen Review is an annual publication by the International Energy Agency that tracks hydrogen production and demand worldwide.

URL: https://assets.bbhub.io/professional/sites/24/hydrogen4.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A line graph illustrates the projected decrease in the levelized cost of hydrogen production from 2023 to 2050 across six countries, highlighting that costs will fall below $1.50 per kilogram by 2030, with China projected to achieve the lowest costs due to advancements in alkaline electrolyzer technology.

enriched_points.point_title: Trends in Levelized Cost of Hydrogen (LCOH)
enriched_points.expanded_content: Recent studies show LCOH for green hydrogen is projected to fall below $1.50/kg by 2030, driven by technological advancements and economies of scale.
Renewable energy costs significantly influence hydrogen production; as solar and wind prices decline, green hydrogen becomes increasingly cost-competitive against fossil fuel sources.
Transitioning to green hydrogen necessitates substantial investments in electrolyzer technology and infrastructure, with initial costs estimated at several billion dollars globally.
